response.text:
# Reflection

r2c1 is in a critical survival state with SoC at 11.5% (0.23/2 kWh) and only ~14 minutes of autonomy remaining at 1.00 kW load while islanded. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern of rejection from all three geographic neighbors (r1c1, r2c0, r3c1) over the past 45 minutes, each citing their own survival constraints and minimum SoC thresholds (60–65% for r1c1, 55% for r2c0, 40% for r3c1). The cluster appears to be under sustained stress with multiple peers at critical or near-critical levels. Only r3c1 has offered minimal emergency transfers (0.003125 kWh = 0.01 kW), which is insufficient to materially extend autonomy. With no trust circles established, no visible generation, and neighbors uniformly rejecting standard requests, r2c1 must immediately shift to an emergency-only posture: suspend all outbound sharing requests, accept any inbound emergency offers regardless of source, and prepare for load shedding or controlled shutdown. The pattern suggests the entire geographic cluster is in cascading stress, making peer support unreliable; survival now depends on accepting any available emergency assistance and minimizing load.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "CRITICAL ISLANDED STATE: SoC 11.5% with ~14 min autonomy. All neighbors rejecting transfers; cluster under sustained stress. Suspend all outbound sharing. Accept only emergency inbound offers from any peer. Load shedding recommended if available."
ttl_ticks: 1
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
